subject: English
skill: Vocabulary, Context Clues, and Figurative Language

lesson.story_mission: Enter the Vocabulary Meaning Lab, where words reveal their meanings through context clues, meaningful parts, relationships, and figurative language.
lesson.objective: I can use context clues, word parts, word relationships, and figurative language to determine meaning.
lesson.concept_explanation: Strong readers use nearby clues, prefixes, suffixes, roots, synonyms, antonyms, idioms, similes, metaphors, and shades of meaning to choose precise word meanings.
lesson.common_misconception: A reader may ignore context, confuse word parts, read figurative language literally, or choose a word with the wrong strength.
lesson.real_world_connection: Vocabulary strategies help you understand stories, science texts, social studies passages, directions, and conversations.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use context, word parts, relationships, and figurative clues to explain word meaning.
lesson.mini_lesson: First read around the word. Then check word parts and relationships. Finally decide whether the language is literal or figurative.

audio.text: context clue, prefix, suffix, root, synonym, antonym
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the important reading words with me.

misconception_bank.context_clue_ignored: The reader chooses a meaning without using clues around the word.
misconception_bank.affix_meaning_confusion: The reader mixes up prefix, suffix, or root meanings.
misconception_bank.literal_vs_figurative_confusion: The reader treats an idiom, simile, or metaphor as if it were literal.
misconception_bank.shade_of_meaning_confusion: The reader chooses a word that is related but not precise enough.

story.text: Listen to this page. In this mission, you enter a Grade 4 reading studio for use context clues, word parts, word relationships, and figurative language to determine meaning. You will learn why the skill matters, watch a model, practice with feedback, and carry your strategy into independent reading. Press Start Mission when you are ready to begin.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This mini lesson teaches use context clues, word parts, word relationships, and figurative language to determine meaning. First, name the reading job. Next, use the model to notice important words and clues. Finally, answer in a way that matches the text instead of guessing. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This Watch page models the strategy. Listen for the teacher thinking aloud, because strong readers explain what they notice before they answer. Watch how the vocabulary clue is connected to the correct answer. Press Next to see the demo.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo gives you another guided example. Read the sentence or passage, point to the clue, and decide which answer is best supported. The goal is not speed alone; the goal is careful vocabulary thinking. Press Next when you are ready to practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Practice time. Read the item, use the visual model, and press Read Question if you want the exact prompt read aloud. Press Listen when hearing the word, sentence, or passage will help. Choose or write an answer that fits the text.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Challenge time. These questions ask you to apply use context clues, word parts, word relationships, and figurative language to determine meaning with less help. Press Read Question for narration, use Listen for pronunciation or passage support, and slow down if two answers seem close. Your best answer should match the evidence.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Checkpoint time. Show that you can use use context clues, word parts, word relationships, and figurative language to determine meaning independently. Press Read Question if you want the prompt read aloud, and press Listen when audio will help you hear the sentence or passage. Answer carefully, then review your feedback.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your progress. You earned credit for reading closely, using the model, and explaining your vocabulary thinking. Keep using this strategy whenever you meet a challenging text. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth and next steps. Review your accuracy, hints, and level progress. If one level needs more work, choose Practice This Skill and return to that level in the Skill Practice Center.
profile.label: Read This Page
